name: kolm
tagline: Your own AI, compiled to your task.
description: kolm is the AI compiler. It turns a task specification into a single signed .kolm artifact: recipes + a verifier + tests + evals + a model pointer, with optional LoRA adapter and vector index. The artifact is portable; the runtime is your choice (customer-hosted bridge or hosted compile). Every inference emits a verifiable HMAC receipt.

concepts.kolm-artifact: Signed zip with rs-1 slots: model.gguf, lora.bin, recipes.json, index.sqlite-vec, verifier.json, manifest.json, signature.sig. In v0.1 these slots ship recipe-tier contents (JSON base-model pointer, KOLMPACK behavior pack, KOLMIDX JSON lookup); native weights, GGUF-LoRA, and sqlite-vec databases are roadmap tiers.
concepts.k-score: K = 0.40*A + 0.15*S + 0.15*L + 0.15*C + 0.15*V where A is held-out accuracy, S is size score, L is latency score, C is cost score, V is recipe coverage. Compile-time gate at 0.85.
concepts.four-ring-chain: HMAC-SHA256 chain: pre-image -> derived -> execution -> seal. Verifiable by the issuer or any holder of the shared tenant receipt secret. Ed25519 public-key receipts and Sigstore Rekor anchoring are roadmap.
concepts.rent-vs-buy: Frontier-API spend captured as (input, verified-output) pairs is distilled into a task-specific local LoRA the customer keeps forever.

provenanceProof.headline: SWE-bench Lite harness ships at kolm bench --reproduce swebench-lite-n150, seed=42, Opus-4.7, swebench 4.1.0 evaluator. Headline lift lands when the first end-to-end signed run completes; no point estimate is published before then.

byokeyPolicy: All tiers bring their own frontier-API key. kolm never resells tokens.
